title:
Small pizza
or a big one?
heroText: Fill the details - see which option gives you more pizza for your money, and exactly how much pizza you’re getting either way.
verdictText: {{percent}}% cheaper per cm<sup>2</sup> than buying {{number}} {{size}} ones.
tieText: Both options cost nearly the same per cm<sup>2</sup>. Pick whichever you can finish.
moreTitle: How much more pizza you get
moreSubtitle: Both pizzas are drawn to scale relative to their total area. The percentage in the middle shows exactly how much more pizza you get with the larger option.
areaTitle: How much pizza you actually get
areaSubtitle: Total area, summed across all pizzas in each option. Bars are far more honest than circles. Your eyes underestimate area differences in circles by 30-40%.
areaTieText: Both options give you essentially the same amount of total pizza. It would take {{ratio}} small pizzas to match 1 big pizza's area.
areaBigWinsText: The big option gives you {{percent}}% more total pizza. It would take {{ratio}} small pizzas to match {{bigCount}} big pizza's area.
areaSmallWinsText: The small option gives you {{percent}}% more total pizza. It would take {{ratio}} small pizzas to match {{bigCount}} big pizza's area.
footer: Math: area = <pi/> x (diameter / 2)<sup>2</sup> | winner is whichever has lower price per area
